aiByPattern.rationale: The class implements a fluent interface for configuring a query and provides a terminal build() method to produce the final string product.

documentationByTarget.builder_terminator_method: The build() method (line 63) acts as the terminal operation, assembling the internal state into the final product string.

aiByPattern.rationale: The class maintains the same interface as the wrapped Repository and is named 'CachedRepository', which is characteristic of a caching proxy rather than an interface adapter.

documentationByTarget.adapter_class: The CachedRepository class (line 93) serves as a proxy that manages access to an underlying Repository instance.

unitTestPlanByTarget.11444108943156767000: Verify that the internal repository reference is correctly initialized and accessible during method calls.
unitTestPlanByTarget.7316032283058600000: Verify that the read method returns the identical value produced by the wrapped Repository's read method.

aiByPattern.rationale: The class wraps a Repository instance and forwards its method calls, adhering to the Decorator pattern's structure for adding behavior (caching) to an existing object.

documentationByTarget.decorator_class: The CachedRepository class (line 93) serves as a decorator that extends the functionality of a Repository object without altering its original source code.
documentationByTarget.decorator_wrapped_component: The constructor at line 95 initializes the wrapped component, allowing the decorator to maintain a reference to the underlying Repository instance.
documentationByTarget.decorator_forwarded_call: The read method at line 97 demonstrates the delegation mechanism, forwarding the request to the wrapped component to ensure interface transparency.

unitTestPlanByTarget.4811460386737542000: Verify that the constructor correctly assigns the provided Repository pointer to the internal member.

aiByPattern.rationale: The class acts as a wrapper around a Repository instance, forwarding the 'read' call to the inner subject, which is the defining characteristic of a Proxy pattern.

documentationByTarget.proxy_class: The CachedRepository class at line 93 serves as the proxy, providing a surrogate interface to the underlying Repository subject.
documentationByTarget.proxy_real_subject: The constructor at line 95 accepts the real subject (Repository) as a dependency, allowing the proxy to delegate operations to it.
documentationByTarget.proxy_forwarded_call: The read method at line 97 implements the delegation logic, forwarding the request to the inner subject to retrieve the requested data.
